title: 10 best herbal teas for bad breath
intro_desc: Herbal teas for bad breath are infusions made from various plants and spices that can help freshen breath and improve oral hygiene. These teas often contain antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties that help to kill the bacteria in the mouth that cause bad breath. Examples of herbal teas that are known to help with bad breath include peppermint, spearmint, sage, cinnamon, and clove. By drinking these teas regularly, individuals can experience fresher breath, improved digestion, and a reduction in the bacteria that cause bad breath. This can lead to increased confidence, better social interactions, and overall improved quality of life.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint tea helps with bad breath because it contains menthol, a compound that has natural antibacterial and antiseptic properties. These properties can help kill the bacteria in your mouth that cause bad breath. Additionally, peppermint tea can freshen your breath by leaving a cool, minty taste in your mouth. Furthermore, peppermint tea is a natural alternative to mouthwashes and breath mints that often contain alcohol and other chemicals that can dry out your mouth and actually make bad breath worse over time. Therefore, drinking peppermint tea can not only help treat bad breath, but also prevent it from occurring in the first place.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial: Peppermint tea contains antimicrobial compounds, such as menthol and limonene, that help reduce the growth of bacteria responsible for bad breath in the mouth.
Anti-inflammatory: The anti-inflammatory properties of peppermint tea, due to rosmarinic acid and flavonoids, can help decrease gum inflammation and redness, which can contribute to bad breath.
Fresh breath: Peppermint tea's refreshing aroma, primarily from menthol, immediately masks bad breath odors and leaves a pleasant, long-lasting minty scent.
Salivation stimulation: Peppermint tea promotes saliva production, which helps wash away bacteria and food particles that cause bad breath.

Digestive aid: Peppermint tea can alleviate digestive issues like bloating, gas, and indigestion, which can indirectly improve bad breath by reducing the production of gases that contribute to halitosis.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in menthol and menthone, which have antibacterial properties that can help freshen breath.
Buds: High in essential oils, which have antimicrobial properties that can help eliminate the cause of bad breath.
Flowers: Contain flavonoids that have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help promote oral health and freshen breath.
Seeds: Contain essential oils that have antiseptic properties, which can help kill the bacteria that cause bad breath.
remedy_recipe: In a teapot, combine 1 cup of boiling water with 1 tablespoon of dried peppermint leaves.
Steep the mixture for 5-10 minutes to allow the active compounds to be released.
Strain the tea to remove the spent peppermint leaves.
Optionally, add a small amount of honey or lemon juice to improve the taste.
Consume the tea slowly, allowing it to coat your mouth and freshen your breath.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm tea helps with bad breath because it contains antimicrobial properties that can help reduce the bacteria in your mouth that cause bad breath. The tea's soothing qualities can also help alleviate any inflammation in the mouth and throat, which can contribute to halitosis. Furthermore, lemon balm is a natural breath freshener, leaving your mouth feeling clean and fresh. Drinking lemon balm tea regularly can help maintain good oral hygiene and keep bad breath at bay.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Lemon balm tea can inhibit the growth of bacteria and other microorganisms that cause bad breath, thanks to its active constituents such as rosmarinic acid and citral.

Antioxidant properties: The high antioxidant content of lemon balm tea, including flavonoids and phenolic acids, can help protect oral tissues from damage and reduce inflammation, contributing to fresher breath.
Anxiolytic properties: Lemon balm tea has calming effects, which can help reduce stress and anxiety, thus preventing excessive mouth-breathing and dry mouth, both of which can lead to bad breath.

Carminative properties: Lemon balm tea can help alleviate digestive issues, such as gas and bloating, which can contribute to bad breath when left untreated. This is due to its active constituents, including citronellal and eugenol.

Antiviral properties: Lemon balm tea can help fight off viral infections, such as cold sores, which can lead to bad breath. This is attributed to its active constituent, citral, which has been shown to have antiviral properties.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of lemon balm tea plant have high concentrations of volatile oils, which can help to eliminate bad breath by killing the bacteria that cause it.
Flowers: The flowers of lemon balm tea plant contain antioxidants and have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce inflammation in the mouth and throat, and prevent bad breath.
Stems: The stems of lemon balm tea plant contain rosmarinic acid, which has been shown to have antibacterial and antiviral properties, making it effective in reducing bad breath.
Seeds: The seeds of lemon balm tea plant are rich in essential oils, which can help to eliminate bad breath by neutralizing the odor caused by bacteria in the mouth.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 tablespoons of fresh lemon balm leaves or use 1 tablespoon of dried leaves.
Rinse the lemon balm leaves under cold water to remove any dirt or impurities.
Boil 1 cup of water and let it cool for 1 minute.
Steep the lemon balm leaves in the hot water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea, discard the leaves, and drink the lemon balm tea up to three times a day for bad breath relief.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Thyme tea helps with bad breath because it contains powerful antiseptic and antibacterial properties that can kill the bacteria in the mouth responsible for causing bad breath. The main active ingredient in thyme is thymol, which has been shown to be effective at reducing oral bacteria and preventing plaque buildup. Additionally, thyme tea has a refreshing and aromatic flavor that can help to mask any lingering unpleasant odors. Drinking thyme tea regularly can help to maintain fresh breath and promote overall oral health.
remedy_properties: Antibacterial: Thyme tea has strong antibacterial properties that help reduce the growth of odor-causing bacteria in the mouth. This is important for bad breath because the bacteria in the mouth are one of the main causes of halitosis. The active constituents responsible for thyme's antibacterial properties include thymol, carvacrol, and cymene.

Antioxidant: Thyme tea is rich in antioxidants, which help protect the mouth and body from damage caused by free radicals. This is important for bad breath because oxidative stress can contribute to the development of halitosis. The main antioxidant compounds found in thyme are rosmarinic acid, luteolin, and apigenin.

Anti-inflammatory: Thyme tea has potent anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce inflammation in the mouth and gums. This is important for bad breath because inflammation can lead to the production of volatile sulfur compounds (VSCs), which are responsible for the foul odor associated with halitosis. The primary anti-inflammatory compounds in thyme are flavonoids, such as apigenin and luteolin.

Antifungal: Thyme tea has been shown to have antifungal properties, which can help control the growth of Candida albicans, a type of yeast that can cause oral thrush and contribute to bad breath. This is important for bad breath because an overgrowth of Candida can lead to the production of unpleasant odors. The active constituents responsible for thyme's antifungal properties include thymol, carvacrol, and linalool.

Astringent: Thyme tea has astringent properties, which can help tighten and tone the gums and oral tissues. This is important for bad breath because tightening the gums can help reduce the spaces where bacteria can hide and cause odors. The astringent properties of thyme are attributed to its high tannin content.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Thyme leaves are rich in antimicrobial compounds that help neutralize the bacteria causing bad breath.
Flowers: Thyme flowers contain essential oils with antibacterial properties, making them effective in reducing oral bacteria that cause bad breath.
Stems: Thyme stems contain tannins that can help reduce inflammation and fight the bacteria causing bad breath.
remedy_recipe: Boil 1 cup of water and add 1 teaspoon of dried thyme leaves, letting it steep for 10 minutes.
Strain the tea to remove the leaves, discarding the solid residue.
Allow the tea to cool down to a drinkable temperature.
Optionally, add a small amount of honey or lemon juice for flavor.
Drink the tea slowly, allowing it to coat your mouth and throat.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sage tea helps with bad breath because it contains antibacterial and astringent properties that help to eliminate the bacteria in the mouth that cause bad breath. The tea's antioxidants also help to reduce inflammation in the mouth and throat, which can contribute to bad breath. Additionally, sage has been shown to have antimicrobial properties that can help to kill off the germs that cause bad breath. Drinking sage tea can help to freshen breath naturally, while also providing soothing relief to a sore or irritated throat.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Sage tea can inhibit the growth of bacteria and other microorganisms in the mouth that cause bad breath, due to the presence of compounds such as camphor, eucalyptol, and thujone.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Sage tea can reduce inflammation in the mouth and throat, which can help to alleviate bad breath caused by inflammation or infection, due to the presence of rosmarinic acid and other phenolic acids.
Astringent properties: Sage tea can help to tighten and tone the tissues in the mouth, reducing the production of excess saliva that can cause bad breath, due to the presence of tannins.

Antioxidant properties: Sage tea can help to neutralize free radicals in the mouth and prevent oxidative stress, which can contribute to bad breath, due to the presence of flavonoids, phenolic acids, and other antioxidant compounds.
Dry mouth relief: Sage tea can help to stimulate the production of saliva and relieve dry mouth, which can cause bad breath, due to the presence of volatile oils such as cineole and limonene.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in antioxidants and antibacterial properties, sage leaves help reduce odor-causing bacteria in the mouth.
Buds: Containing essential oils, sage buds have potent antibacterial and anti-inflammatory effects, which aid in freshening breath.
Stems: High in tannins, sage stems provide astringent properties, helping to control excess oil production and reducing bad breath.
remedy_recipe: Boil one cup of water in a saucepan for 3-5 minutes.
Add one teaspoon of dried sage leaves or one tablespoon of fresh sage leaves to the boiling water.
Reduce heat and let the mixture simmer for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup, discarding the sage leaves.
Allow the tea to cool slightly before drinking, preferably sipping slowly after meals.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Oregano tea helps with bad breath because it contains compounds with antimicrobial properties, such as carvacrol and thymol. These compounds can help to reduce the growth of bacteria in the mouth that cause bad breath. Additionally, oregano tea can help to stimulate the production of saliva, which can help to wash away food particles and bacteria that can contribute to bad breath. The tea's anti-inflammatory properties may also help to soothe a sore throat or inflamed gums, which can be a source of bad breath. Furthermore, oregano tea has a strong, aromatic flavor that can help to mask bad breath temporarily.
remedy_properties: Antibacterial properties: Oregano tea can inhibit the growth of bacteria that cause bad breath, thanks to its high concentration of thymol and carvacrol.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Oregano tea can reduce inflammation in the mouth and throat, which can help alleviate bad breath caused by inflammation and infection.
Antioxidant properties: Oregano tea contains powerful antioxidants that can neutralize free radicals in the body, reducing oxidative stress that can contribute to bad breath.
Immune-boosting properties: Oregano tea can help strengthen the immune system, which can help the body fight off infections that can cause bad breath.

Digestive properties: Oregano tea can improve digestion and reduce gas and bloating, which can help prevent bad breath caused by digestive issues. The active constituents responsible for these properties include thymol, carvacrol, and other volatile oils.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Often used in medicinal teas for bad breath due to their antibacterial properties that can help reduce oral bacteria.
Flowers: Contain antioxidants and essential oils that can help eliminate bad breath caused by toxins and infections.
Seeds: Rich in thymol and carvacrol, which have antimicrobial properties that can freshen breath and promote oral health.
remedy_recipe: In a teapot, combine 1 teaspoon of dried oregano leaves with 8 ounces of boiling water.
Allow the mixture to steep for 5-10 minutes to extract the herb's oils.
Strain the tea to remove the oregano leaves, then pour the liquid into a cup.
Optionally, add honey or lemon to taste.
Sip the tea slowly, allowing it to coat your mouth and freshen your breath.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Rosemary tea helps with bad breath because it contains antibacterial and antimicrobial properties that combat the growth of bacteria in the mouth, which is a common cause of bad breath. The tea's anti-inflammatory properties can also help reduce inflammation in the gums, providing additional relief from bad breath. Moreover, rosemary tea is a natural breath freshener, leaving a pleasant and long-lasting smell in the mouth. Its refreshing and invigorating taste makes it an excellent alternative to traditional mouthwashes and breath fresheners. Drinking rosemary tea regularly can help keep your breath fresh and your mouth healthy.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial: Rosemary tea contains antimicrobial compounds, such as camphor and cineole, that help reduce the growth of bacteria responsible for causing bad breath.
Anti-inflammatory: The anti-inflammatory properties of rosemary tea, due to its rosmarinic acid content, can help decrease inflammation in the mouth and reduce bad breath caused by inflamed gums.
Antioxidant: Rosemary tea is rich in antioxidants, including carnosol and carnosic acid, which help protect oral tissues from damage and support overall oral health, reducing bad breath.
Saliva stimulation: Drinking rosemary tea can help stimulate saliva production, which is important for washing away food particles and bacteria that can cause bad breath.
Deodorizing: The deodorizing properties of rosemary tea, attributed to its chlorophyll content, help neutralize unpleasant odors in the mouth, providing immediate relief from bad breath.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of the rosemary tea plant have antimicrobial properties that can help to reduce the bacteria in the mouth that cause bad breath.
Flowers: The flowers of the rosemary tea plant have a pleasant aroma that can help to freshen breath and provide a sweet taste to the tea.
Stems: The stems of the rosemary tea plant contain essential oils that have antiseptic properties, which can help to kill off bacteria that cause bad breath.
Seeds: The seeds of the rosemary tea plant contain high levels of antioxidants that can help to reduce inflammation in the mouth and throat, which can contribute to bad breath.
remedy_recipe: Boil 1 cup of water in a saucepan for 3-5 minutes.
Add 1 teaspoon of dried rosemary leaves to the boiling water.
Reduce heat and let the mixture simmer for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup, discarding the rosemary leaves.
Sip the hot tea slowly, allowing it to swish around in your mouth for added breath-freshening benefits.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Cinnamon tea helps with bad breath because it contains antibacterial and antimicrobial properties that combat the growth of odor-causing bacteria in the mouth. The main active component in cinnamon, called cinnamaldehyde, is responsible for its potent antibacterial effects. Furthermore, cinnamon tea's warm temperature and astringent properties can help increase saliva production, which naturally washes away bacteria and food particles that can lead to bad breath. Additionally, cinnamon's refreshing and aromatic scent can temporarily mask any lingering unpleasant odors, leaving your breath smelling fresh and clean.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial: Cinnamon tea inhibits the growth of microorganisms that cause bad breath, such as Streptococcus mutans, due to the presence of active compounds like cinnamaldehyde and eugenol.

Anti-inflammatory: Its anti-inflammatory properties reduce inflammation in the mouth and gums, which can contribute to bad breath, thanks to the presence of bioactive compounds like cinnamaldehyde and procyanidins.

Antioxidant: Cinnamon tea contains high levels of antioxidants that help neutralize free radicals and protect oral tissues from damage, thus preventing bad breath caused by cellular damage, due to the presence of polyphenols and flavonoids.
Saliva stimulation: Cinnamon tea promotes saliva production, which helps wash away bacteria and food particles that cause bad breath, due to the aromatic compounds found in cinnamon.
Soothing properties: Cinnamon tea can help soothe irritated mouth and throat tissues, reducing inflammation and irritation that can cause bad breath, due to the presence of volatile oils and tannins.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in antioxidants and essential oils, leaves help neutralize bad breath by eliminating bacteria and reducing inflammation.
Bark: Contains cinnamaldehyde, an effective antibacterial compound that fights bad breath by reducing the growth of oral bacteria.
Buds: Packed with essential oils and antioxidants, buds provide a potent source for reducing bad breath, inhibiting the growth of odor-causing microorganisms.
Seeds: High in antimicrobial properties, seeds help freshen breath by eliminating bacteria and promoting overall oral health.
remedy_recipe: Boil one cup of water and add 1 teaspoon of cinnamon powder or a cinnamon stick.
Reduce heat and let it simmer for 10-15 minutes to allow the cinnamon to release its beneficial compounds.
Remove from heat and add freshly squeezed juice of half a lemon (optional for added flavor and vitamin C).
Strain the tea to remove cinnamon pieces and let it cool down to a drinkable temperature.
Sip the tea slowly, allowing it to coat your mouth and reduce bad breath caused by bacteria.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tasmanian blue gum tea helps with bad breath because it contains antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties that fight against the bacteria causing bad breath. The tea is made from the leaves of the Tasmanian blue gum tree, which has high levels of eucalyptus oil. This oil has been shown to have antibacterial effects that can kill off the bacteria in the mouth that cause bad breath. Additionally, the tea's anti-inflammatory properties can help reduce any swelling or irritation in the gums, which can also contribute to bad breath. Drinking Tasmanian blue gum tea regularly can help maintain fresh breath and promote overall oral health.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial: Tasmanian blue gum tea has antimicrobial properties that help reduce the growth of odor-causing bacteria in the mouth. This is important for bad breath because the bacteria in the mouth are one of the primary causes of halitosis. The active constituents responsible for these antimicrobial properties include terpinen-4-ol, α-pinene, and γ-terpinene.

Anti-inflammatory: The tea has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation in the gums and mouth, which can contribute to bad breath. This property is important because inflammation can lead to the production of volatile sulfur compounds (VSCs), which are primary contributors to halitosis. The active constituents responsible for these anti-inflammatory properties include 1,8-cineole, α-pinene, and β-pinene.

Antioxidant: Tasmanian blue gum tea contains antioxidants that help protect the cells in the mouth from damage caused by free radicals. This property is important for bad breath because oxidative stress can lead to an imbalance in the oral microbiome, which can result in halitosis. The active constituents responsible for these antioxidant properties include gallic acid, catechins, and quercetin.

Antiseptic: The tea has antiseptic properties that help prevent the growth of bacteria and other microorganisms in the mouth. This property is important for bad breath because it helps maintain a healthy oral environment, which is crucial for preventing halitosis. The active constituents responsible for these antiseptic properties include terpinen-4-ol, α-pinene, and γ-terpinene.

Astringent: Tasmanian blue gum tea has astringent properties that help tighten and tone the gums and oral tissues. This property is important for bad breath because it can help remove food particles and debris from the mouth, which can contribute to halitosis. The active constituents responsible for these astringent properties include tannins, flavonoids, and saponins.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of the Tasmanian blue gum tea plant have antimicrobial properties that can help to eliminate the bacteria in the mouth that cause bad breath.
Bark: The bark of the Tasmanian blue gum tea plant contains tannins that have astringent properties, which can help to reduce inflammation in the mouth and eliminate bad breath.
Flowers: The flowers of the Tasmanian blue gum tea plant have antiseptic properties that can help to kill the bacteria that cause bad breath and promote oral health.
Seeds: The seeds of the Tasmanian blue gum tea plant contain essential oils that have a refreshing scent, which can help to mask bad breath and leave the mouth feeling fresh and clean.
remedy_recipe: Boil 1 cup of water and add 1 teaspoon of dried Tasmanian blue gum leaves, allowing the mixture to steep for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the tea to remove leaves, discarding the solid residue.
Let the tea cool down to a drinkable temperature.
Drink the tea slowly, swishing it around in your mouth before swallowing.
Repeat this process 2-3 times a day for best results.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Spearmint tea helps with bad breath because it contains antibacterial and antimicrobial properties that can kill the bacteria in your mouth responsible for causing bad breath. The menthol present in spearmint tea also provides a refreshing and cooling sensation, temporarily masking any unpleasant odors. Moreover, spearmint tea can stimulate the production of saliva, which helps to wash away food particles and bacteria, further preventing bad breath. Regular consumption of spearmint tea can therefore promote oral hygiene and freshen your breath naturally.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Spearmint tea inhibits the growth of bacteria and microorganisms in the mouth that cause bad breath, due to the presence of menthol and limonene.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Spearmint tea reduces inflammation in the mouth and gums, which can help alleviate bad breath caused by gum diseases, due to the presence of rosmarinic acid.

Antioxidant properties: Spearmint tea contains high levels of antioxidants that help neutralize free radicals and reduce oxidative stress, which can contribute to bad breath, due to the presence of rosmarinic acid and catechins.
Digestive properties: Spearmint tea promotes digestion and reduces stomach upsets, which can help alleviate bad breath caused by indigestion, due to the presence of carvone and limonene.
Deodorizing properties: Spearmint tea has a natural, fresh and strong aroma that helps to neutralize and cover up bad breath, due to the presence of menthol and limonene.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of the spearmint plant have oils that contain antiseptic properties, helping to kill bacteria that cause bad breath.
Flowers: Spearmint flowers have menthol, which provides a refreshing and cooling effect in the mouth, temporarily masking bad breath.
Seeds: Spearmint seeds contain thymol, a compound that has antimicrobial properties, aiding in reducing bad breath caused by bacteria.
remedy_recipe: Measure - Boil 1 cup of water and add 1 teaspoon of dried spearmint leaves or 1 tablespoon of fresh spearmint leaves.
Steep - Let the mixture steep for 5-10 minutes, depending on how strong you like your tea.
Strain - Strain the tea into a cup, discarding the used spearmint leaves.
Sweeten - Add honey or lemon to taste, if desired.
Serve - Enjoy your homemade herbal spearmint tea while it's still warm, up to three times a day after meals to help freshen breath.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Narrow-leaved paperbark tea helps with bad breath because it contains antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties that can eliminate the bacteria causing bad breath. The tea is made from the bark of the Melaleuca squamea tree, which is rich in essential oils, flavonoids, and tannins. These compounds work together to reduce the growth of odor-causing microorganisms in the mouth, thus freshening the breath. Additionally, the tea's astringent properties can help tighten and cleanse the gums, reducing the risk of gum disease and bad breath. Drinking narrow-leaved paperbark tea regularly can help maintain oral hygiene and promote fresh breath.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial: Narrow-leaved paperbark tea inhibits the growth of bacteria and fungi responsible for bad breath, as the plant contains volatile oils such as 1,8-cineole and alpha-pinene.

Anti-inflammatory: The tea's ability to reduce inflammation in the mouth and throat helps decrease bad breath caused by inflammation and irritation, thanks to the presence of compounds like terpinen-4-ol and eucalyptol.

Antioxidant: Narrow-leaved paperbark tea contains high levels of antioxidants that help protect oral tissues from damage, reducing the risk of conditions leading to bad breath, due to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic acids.

Expectorant: The tea's expectorant properties help clear mucus and phlegm from the respiratory tract, reducing the chances of halitosis caused by post-nasal drip, as a result of compounds like cineole and alpha-pinene.

Soothing: Narrow-leaved paperbark tea soothes irritated mucous membranes in the mouth and throat, alleviating dry mouth and providing relief from bad breath associated with it, owing to the presence of tannins and mucilaginous compounds.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in antimicrobial compounds, leaves of the narrow-leaved paperbark tea plant can help reduce the growth of bacteria causing bad breath.
Bark: The bark contains tannins that have astringent properties, helping to control excess oil production in the mouth which can lead to bad breath.
Stems: High in antioxidants, stems can help neutralize free radicals that can contribute to bad breath by damaging oral tissues.
Flowers: With natural anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, flowers can help soothe irritations in the mouth and throat, reducing bad breath caused by inflammation.
Seeds: Seeds are a great source of essential oils that can help eliminate the bacteria causing bad breath and leave a fresh, pleasant scent in the mouth.
remedy_recipe: Pick 2-3 narrow-leaved paperbark (Melaleuca squamea) leaves, ensuring they are free from dirt and insects.
Rinse the leaves under cold running water, then tear them into small pieces and place them in a teapot.
Boil 2 cups of water and pour it over the paperbark leaves in the teapot.
Allow the tea to steep for 5-10 minutes to extract the beneficial compounds and obtain a brownish-yellow color.
Strain the tea into a cup, discarding the leaves, and drink it warm, up to 2-3 times a day after meals to help freshen breath.
